hero.title: Beyond Adoption
hero.description: An account from someone who was <1>adopted at 10 months old</1>, with questions that help <3>demystify adoption</3>. An honest invitation: adopting does not guarantee a "happily ever after" — it is the <5>beginning of a journey</5> of challenges, discoveries, and daily learning.
hero.quote: "Adopting is starting a new story — with truths that deserve to be spoken aloud."

narrative.title: Demystifying adoption with a true story
narrative.p1: This is not a cold manual: it's a <1>personal account</1> traversed by <3>20 questions</3> that clarify real doubts about the adoption process — for those thinking about adopting, families living this choice, and anyone who wants to understand without romanticizing.
narrative.p2: The book shows that <1>adopting does not automatically mean a "happily ever after"</1> — and that this doesn't diminish love. It means recognizing the <3>start of a journey</3> marked by challenges, discoveries, and lessons that renew every day.
narrative.p3: In Portuguese, in an agile format for straightforward reading: ideal for those seeking <1>clarity, warmth, and information</1> without fairy tale filters.

duality.title: What you take from this book
duality.subtitle: Beauty and complexity walk together. Knowing what to expect — including what doesn't come ready in a dream package — is the first step towards a more conscious and sustainable adoption.
duality.shadowsTitle: What the book does not promise

lights.title: Truth with warmth
lights.desc: A lived account that names feelings and situations many families recognize but rarely see in books.

lights.title: 20 clarifying questions
lights.desc: Direct answers to common questions about adoption — without judgment, with information and sensitivity.

lights.title: For parents, future parents, and the curious
lights.desc: Useful for those in the process, those who have adopted, and those who want to understand adoption beyond clichés.

shadows.title: Not a fairy tale promise
shadows.desc: The book does not sell an automatic happy ending: it talks about grief, expectations, and adjustments — because bonds are built over time.

shadows.title: Identity and belonging
shadows.desc: Touches on real issues of those born in one story and growing up in another — with honesty.

shadows.title: Bonds require presence
shadows.desc: Love and choice do not cancel out challenges; this book helps see the journey with less guilt and more clarity.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Questions about the content, the book's message, and purchasing. The full 20 questions are in the ebook.

items.q: Is the book Beyond Adoption fiction or a real account?
items.a: It is a personal account from someone adopted at 10 months old, combined with 20 questions that help demystify the adoption process — information and experience, without empty romanticization.

items.q: How many questions about adoption does the book answer?
items.a: There are 20 questions, designed to clarify common doubts and bring readers closer to the reality of adoption, with accessible language.

items.q: Does the ebook say adoption is always 'happily ever after'?
items.a: No. The book makes it clear that adopting doesn't automatically guarantee a fairy tale ending — and presents adoption as the start of a journey with challenges, discoveries, and daily learning.

items.q: Where can I buy the Beyond Adoption (Kindle) book?
items.a: On Amazon, by ASIN B0GTTR8138 — Portuguese eBook, with Page Flip and screen reader compatibility. Use the 'Buy on Amazon' button on this page to go straight to the product page.

items.q: What is the price of the ebook?
items.a: The value is set on Amazon and may vary by promotions. Always check the product page (ASIN B0GTTR8138) for the current price.

items.q: When is the physical version coming out?
items.a: The idea is to release the physical version within a year after the ebook launch. The physical edition should bring extras and, in some cases, the option to buy an autographed copy — follow the author's updates.

items.q: Is there a difference between the ebook and the future physical version?
items.a: Yes: the physical version is planned with extra materials and the possibility of an autographed kit in channels indicated by the author.

items.q: Who is the book suitable for?
items.a: For couples and families considering adoption, those who have already adopted, professionals who follow families, and anyone who wants to understand adoption more deeply and with fewer myths — especially in the context of parenting and relationships.

author.title: A voice born from this story
author.p1: I was <1>adopted at 10 months old</1> — and this mark traverses everything I write. Not to glamorize nor to dramatize: to <3>name the real</3>, welcome those in the middle of the process, and offer questions and answers I wish I had found earlier.
author.p2: My mission with "Beyond Adoption" is for the word <1>adoption</1> to be associated with <3>truth, care, and realistic hope</3> — never replacing one family with another as if swapping parts, but recognizing a bond that is chosen and rebuilt every day.

nextBooks.title: More stories are on the way
nextBooks.p1: <1>Beyond Adoption</1> does not close the door on what's still to come. I continue to write with the same curiosity and responsibility: each new text is born from real conversations, study time, and living alongside families and narratives that asked to be told with care — without a rush that sacrifices the truth.
nextBooks.p2: The <1>next books</1> emerge gradually: some are already in preparation or revision; others are still finding the right tone. They will be <3>published as they find the right format and timing</3>, always with the commitment to combine accounts, information, and listening, in the same spirit as this first step.
nextBooks.p3: If this book spoke to you, stay tuned to the publisher's news and the author's channels: more chapters of this journey are on the way.

education.title: Courses that traverse what I write
education.subtitle: To support text and account with rigor, I sought training that dialogues with childhood, adolescence, family, and rights. Below are the courses I took — each left marks on how I read the world and choose every word.
courses: Introduction to adoption of children and adolescents
Introduction to infractions and socio-educational measures
